title: BAKER COUNTY CRISIS LINE - 211info
text: NEW DIRECTIONS NORTHWEST INC OUTPATIENT PROGRAM BAKER COUNTY CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for individuals experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises through a county crisis line. 3425 13TH Street Baker City, OR 97814 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 519-7126 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Crisis Line (541) 519-7126 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for services FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None LANGUAGES: English, interpretation or signing by arrangement AREA SERVED: Baker County SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Exit 304 off of I-84 Services: General Crisis Intervention Hotlines Mental Health Crisis Lines Suicide Prevention Hotlines Other Services Offered At This Location: COUNSELING SETTINGS - OUTPATIENT MENTAL HEALTH AND SUD ADDICTIONS EMPLOYMENT - SUPPORTED EMPLOYMENT MENTAL HEALTH SUPPORT SERVICES - EARLY ASSESSMENT AND SUPPORT ALLIANCE REHABILITATION/HABILITATION SERVICES - COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES PROGRAM SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES - ASSERTIVE COMMUNITY TREATMENT If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
